input_row.title: Ensure that a Microsoft Entra diagnostic setting exists to send Microsoft Graph activity logs to an appropriate destination
input_row.rationale: Ensure that a Microsoft Entra diagnostic setting is configured to send Microsoft Graph activity logs to a suitable destination, such as a Log Analytics workspace, storage account, or event hub. This enables centralized monitoring and analysis of all HTTP requests that the Microsoft Graph service receives and processes for a tenant.
input_row.audit: Microsoft Graph activity logs provide visibility into HTTP requests made to the Microsoft Graph service, helping detect unauthorized access, suspicious activity, and security threats. Configuring diagnostic settings in Microsoft Entra ensures these logs are collected and sent to an appropriate destination for monitoring, analysis, and retention. Impact: A Microsoft Entra ID P1 or P2 tenant license is required to access the Microsoft Graph activity logs. The amount of data logged and, thus, the cost incurred can vary significantly depending on the tenant size and the applications in your tenant that interact with the Microsoft Graph APIs. See the following pricing calculations for respective services: • Log Analytics: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/cost- logs#pricing-model. • Azure Storage: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-gb/pricing/details/storage/blobs/. • Event Hubs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-gb/pricing/details/event-hubs/
input_row.remediation: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to Microsoft Entra ID. 2. Under Monitoring, click Diagnostic settings. 3. Next to each diagnostic setting, click Edit setting, and review the selected log categories and destination details. 4. Ensure that at least one diagnostic setting is configured to send MicrosoftGraphActivityLogs to an appropriate destination.
input_row.impact: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to Microsoft Entra ID. 2. Under Monitoring, click Diagnostic settings. 3. Click + Add diagnostic setting. 4. Provide a Diagnostic setting name. 5. Under Logs > Categories, check the box next to MicrosoftGraphActivityLogs. 6. Configure an appropriate destination for the logs. 7. Click Save. Default Value: By default, Microsoft Entra diagnostic settings do not exist. References: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/monitoring-health/howto- configure-diagnostic-settings 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/microsoft-graph-activity-logs-overview 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/cost-logs#pricing- model 4. https://azure.microsoft.com/en-gb/pricing/details/storage/blobs/ 5. https://azure.microsoft.com/en-gb/pricing/details/event-hubs/
input_row.reference: A Microsoft Entra ID P1 or P2 tenant license is required to access the Microsoft Graph activity logs. The amount of data logged and, thus, the cost incurred can vary significantly depending on the tenant size and the applications in your tenant that interact with the Microsoft Graph APIs. See the following pricing calculations for respective services: • Log Analytics: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-monitor/logs/cost- logs#pricing-model. • Azure Storage: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-gb/pricing/details/storage/blobs/. • Event Hubs: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-gb/pricing/details/event-hubs/

gpt_response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:** Based on the provided information, the control can be fully automated via Azure Resource Manager APIs. The rationale clearly outlines the logging mechanism which is specifically handled through Azure APIs related to diagnostic settings.

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_entra_diagnostic_setting_graph_activity_logs_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
1. **Azure Resource Manager APIs or Azure CLI commands to use:**
   - Use the Azure REST API endpoint to check the diagnostic settings:
     ```
     GET https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.Entra/identity/{entaId}/diagnosticSettings?api-version=2021-12-01
     ```
   - Alternatively, you can use the Azure CLI command:
     ```
     az monitor diagnostic-settings list --resource {entaId}
     ```

2. **Specific resources to enumerate:**
   - The resource type is Microsoft Entra/identity. You'll need to specify the subscription ID and resource group name which contains the Entra resource.

3. **Exact configuration properties/settings to validate:**
   - For each diagnostic setting, check if `logs` includes `MicrosoftGraphActivityLogs` and verify if it has an appropriate destination configured (Log Analytics workspace, storage account, or event hub).

4. **Clear Pass/Fail criteria with expected values:**
   - **Pass:** At least one diagnostic setting is found that sends `MicrosoftGraphActivityLogs` to a valid destination.
   - **Fail:** No such diagnostic setting exists or the required log category is not selected.

5. **Example API endpoint or CLI command:** 
   - The GET endpoint provided above can be used to check the configuration directly.
   - The command in Azure CLI will give you a JSON output detailing the existing diagnostic settings.

With this automated approach, organizations can efficiently ensure that diagnostic settings for Microsoft Graph activity logs are properly configured without needing manual intervention.
